lead_profile_text: Currently based in Indonesia, back in UK for ~1 month. LTL landlord for 4 years — most recent tenant just left. Property currently vacant and unfurnished. Competing LTL offer live (interested party). Recurring shower pump issue (replaced 6 times in 4 years). Mortgage £600/month.
emotional_profile: Motivated but cautious. Time-pressured by imminent return to Indonesia. Competing LTL offer is live risk. Needs complete hands-off management reassurance and full cost transparency.
delay_trigger_phrases: shower pump's been replaced six times in four years
electric shower installation recommended before listing
delay_timeline: Must resolve within Laura's UK stay — before June 30
green_light_trigger_phrases: I'm only here for about a month
decision deadline aligns with Laura's UK stay before returning to Indonesia

top_objections.text: How do I manage the property when I'm in Indonesia?
top_objections.zac_response: Complete remote operation — Slack, monthly statements, £300 maintenance cap without approval needed

top_objections.text: What about the shower and water tank recurring issues?
top_objections.zac_response: Electric shower installation recommended pre-listing; Stayful can coordinate contractor

top_objections.text: How do the costs work — what do I actually net after all deductions?
top_objections.zac_response: Full cost breakdown: STL £1,742/month net vs LTL £644/month — even September worst month beats LTL

top_objections.text: There's a tenant interested in the property
top_objections.zac_response: All 12 STL months net above LTL including September (£800 STL vs £644 LTL)

top_questions.answer: Complete remote operation — Stayful handles all guest management, maintenance coordination (£300 cap), monthly statements and Slack updates

top_questions.text: What needs to happen before the property can list?
top_questions.answer: Electric shower installation, furnishing required — Stayful can assist with checklist and contractor coordination

top_questions.text: What is the income comparison vs continuing with LTL?
top_questions.answer: STL £1,742/month net vs LTL £644/month net — uplift £1,098/month; all 12 months net above LTL even in worst month (Sep: £800 vs £644)

what_moved_them: All 12 months net above LTL — even September at £800 vs LTL £644
Maintenance transparency — up to £300 without approval needed
Stayful handles everything remotely — no physical presence required
Special offer of 13% saving vs standard 15%
what_fell_flat: Competing LTL tenant offer not fully displaced — still live risk
Shower/water tank recurring issue creates legitimate pre-listing barrier

conversion_gaps.gap: Competing LTL offer is live — real alternative with interested tenant
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Emphasise all-12-months-above-LTL chart; worst month £800 vs LTL £644 — STL wins every single month

conversion_gaps.gap: Property not ready to list — furnishing and shower fix required
conversion_gaps.how_to_fill: Send setup quote and contractor coordination offer; Stayful can manage setup before she returns to Indonesia

offer_framing: 13% + VAT if signed by June 30 — framed around Laura's UK departure deadline as a natural and genuine close window
zac_close_phrase: Sign before June 30 — we can coordinate the shower fix and setup while you're still here; by the time you land in Indonesia it's running
zac_income_intro: STL £1,742/month net vs your current LTL £644/month. Even your worst month on STL — September at £800 — beats what you'd get from a long-term tenant.
zac_transition_phrases: The key thing for your situation is that you never need to be here
Even in the worst month — September — you net £800 versus £644 on a long let
zac_reassurance_phrases: Up to £300 maintenance — we handle it and tell you after; above £300 we check with you first
Monthly statement, Slack channel — you'll always know exactly what's happening from Indonesia
